title: user — tenancy hooks
description: This module IS tenant-aware for TenantMember + User. It participates in tenancy lifecycle via two hooks. Distinct from `listeners.json` (which is domain-event listeners) — tenancy hooks fire at specific tenancy lifecycle points (bootstrapped / provisioning / erasure).

reference.hierarchy_note: See `hierarchy.md` §12 + `tenancy-columns.md` for the tenant plane the user module lives on.

hooks.purpose: When a new Tenant is provisioned, seed the initial TenantMember for the tenant's owner User — role=owner, is_default=true. Skipped when the caller already provided a membership (idempotent). This is the tenancy-hook counterpart of the listener in `listeners.json` — the hook variant lets the tenancy module orchestrate a transaction across every registered module (tenant erasure needs cross-module knowledge to unwind cleanly).

Runs BEFORE tenancy's FK cascade drops the tenant. Iterates every TenantMember row on the tenant + for each User where the membership is_default=true AND the User has other memberships on OTHER tenants, promote the oldest sibling to default. This preserves the invariant `every User has a default tenant` during the erasure window.

notes: `hooks.json` and `listeners.json` are BOTH legitimate. Hooks are tenancy-lifecycle-specific + participate in the tenancy transaction; listeners are general domain-event subscribers. Some hooks are also exposed as listeners (see SeedOwnerMembershipOnTenantProvisioned in both files) — tenancy dispatches the event AND runs the hook; whichever runs first wins the idempotency check.

This module does NOT participate in tenancy's `Bootstrappers` (which run per-tenant setup like cache prefix swap) because User + Profile + TenantMember rows are the tenant's data, not tenant-scoped runtime config.
See `.kiro/steering/hierarchy.md` §12 for the split between bootstrappers (per-request tenant switch) and hooks (lifecycle events).
